name: Southern Maryland Tri-County Community Action
description_short: Non-profit community action agency providing free housing counseling, food assistance, job training, and early childhood education to low-income residents across three Maryland counties.
description_long:
Southern Maryland Tri-County Community Action Committee, Inc. (SMTCCAC) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization incorporated in August 1965, founded by concerned Calvert County residents responding to local poverty. Operating across Calvert, Charles, and St. Mary's counties in Maryland, SMTCCAC serves eligible low-income families and individuals with comprehensive social services designed to reduce poverty and promote self-sufficiency.

SMTCCAC offers a diverse portfolio of free and low-cost services including housing counseling and emergency services, Head Start and Early Head Start early childhood education programs, the Emergency Food Assistance Program (TEFAP), Commercial Driver's License training, diaper distribution with family support services, and Friendly Health Services Adult Medical Day Care. All programs are designed for income-qualified families and individuals, with educational programs serving multiple sites across the three-county service area. The organization emphasizes family engagement, health screenings, and community referrals alongside direct assistance.

What distinguishes SMTCCAC is its integrated approach combining emergency assistance with long-term skill development and upward mobility services. The organization explicitly connects basic needs programs (food, diapers, emergency housing) with career training (CDL program) and childhood education, recognizing that poverty alleviation requires multi-faceted intervention. The agency is an equal opportunity provider and actively recruits volunteers, emphasizing community engagement and outcome-driven service delivery across its core values of excellence, integrity, and accountability.

As a legitimate non-profit with established community roots since 1965, SMTCCAC provides genuine free services without predatory lending or hidden fees. The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are restricted to three Maryland counties—and program eligibility requires income qualification. The organization appears to operate efficiently as a traditional community action agency but does not offer credit repair, debt relief, or personal lending services.
pros: Legitimate 501(c)(3) non-profit established in 1965 with deep community roots and demonstrated track record
Comprehensive free early childhood education (Head Start/Early Head Start) with research-based curriculum and health screenings
Emergency Food Assistance Program (TEFAP) provides direct food support with volunteer opportunities for community engagement
Career development through free Commercial Driver's License training program—directly addresses employment barriers
Free housing counseling and emergency services division dedicated to low-income families
Integrated family support services connecting diaper distribution with job training, education, and housing assistance
No predatory fees, loans, or credit-building schemes—purely assistance-based model
cons: Geographic limitation: Services only available to residents of Calvert, Charles, and St. Mary's counties in Maryland
Income qualification requirements restrict eligibility; exact thresholds not detailed on website
Limited information about application process, wait times, and current enrollment capacity on primary website
Does not offer credit repair, debt counseling, or financial literacy programs—focused on basic needs and employment
Adult Medical Day Care service is described as 'opening soon' with limited operational details available
best_for: Low-income families in Southern Maryland seeking free early childhood education and family support services
Individuals pursuing commercial driver's license training as a pathway to employment
Families needing emergency food assistance, rental support, or housing counseling in the tri-county area
Parents of infants and toddlers eligible for Early Head Start programs with comprehensive family engagement
diagnosis: SMTCCAC is best for income-qualified low-income families and individuals in Southern Maryland (Calvert, Charles, St. Mary's counties) seeking free emergency assistance, childhood education, or career training through a legitimate non-profit. Critical caveat: This is a traditional community action agency providing basic needs assistance and skill development, not credit repair, debt relief, or financial lending services—it serves a fundamentally different purpose than commercial consumer finance companies.
services: Head Start early childhood education program with multiple county locations
Early Head Start services for infants and toddlers
Emergency Food Assistance Program (TEFAP) with volunteer food distribution
Housing counseling services through Division of Housing and Emergency Services
Emergency rental assistance and housing services for low-income families
Commercial Driver's License (CDL) training and test preparation
Free diaper distribution connected to family support services
Friendly Health Services Adult Medical Day Care (nursing care, medication support, meals, activities, transportation)
Community referral services and social services coordination
Health and dental screenings for Head Start/Early Head Start participants
Job training and educational support programs
Volunteer opportunities and community engagement initiatives
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
